---
title: "AI in Research & Social Care"
subtitle: "Moving Beyond the Chatbot"
author: "Mustafa Aslan"
date: "April 24, 2025"
format:
    revealjs:
        css: style.css
        transition: "fade"
        width: 1920
        height: 1080
        footer: "[AI and AI in Research](https://mustafaslancoto.github.io/talks/presentations/qqf.html)"
        slide-number: c/t
        preview-links: auto
        html-math-method: mathjax
        # title-slide-attributes:
        #     data-background-image: "images/ai.jpeg"
        #     data-background-size: cover
---

# Outline

:::{style="color: #091717; font-size: 1.5em; font-weight: bold;"}
- What is AI?
- AI in Research
- Key Generative AI Tools for Researchers
- Social Care Use Cases
- Predicting Future Care Demand
- Critical Considerations
:::


# What is AI? <br> (It's not just ChatGPT/Gemini/Copilot)

## [AI is a Toolbox, Not a Magic Wand]{style="color: #091717"} {background-image="images/ai.jpeg" background-size="cover"}


## Main Engines of AI

:::{style="color: #091717; font-size: 1.3em"}
Most people see **Generative AI**, but the engine of research is often:

:::{.incremental}

* **Machine Learning (ML):** Teaching computers to learn from data to make predictions.
* **Forecasting:** Predicting future trends based on historical data and patterns.
* **Optimization:** Solving complex "best-fit" problems.
* **Natural Language Processing (NLP):** Understanding and generating human language.
* **Computer Vision:** Analyzing images and videos for insights.
* **Reinforcement Learning:** Training agents to make decisions through trial and error.
* **Generative AI:** Creating new content (text, images, code) based on learned patterns using large language models (LLMs).
:::
:::

# AI in Research

## AI as a Research Partner/Assistant, not a Replacement

<br>
<br>

> "AI doesn't replace the researcher; it removes the friction of the mundane."

### Key Applications

1. **Data Synthesis:** Mapping 20 years of research in seconds.
2. **Pattern Discovery:** Finding signals and patterns in complex datasets that humans might miss.
3. **Automated Workflows:** Managing large-scale longitudinal studies.
4. **Hypothesis Generation:** Suggesting new research directions based on existing data.


---

## Key Generative AI Tools for Researchers

<br>

:::{.fragment}
[**🔬 Perplexity AI**](https://www.perplexity.ai/)

-  Real-time research synthesis with citations, fact-checking, and current information retrieval for literature review

:::


:::{.fragment}
[**📚 SciSpace (Typeset)**](https://www.typeset.io/)

-  AI-powered paper analysis and understanding: break down complex papers, extract key findings, answer questions

:::    

:::{.fragment}
[**📖 NotebookLM**](https://notebooklm.google.com/)

- Transform research documents into interactive conversations, study guides, and podcast-style discussions
:::

:::{.fragment}

[**🤖 ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Copilot**](https://gemini.google.com/app)

- General-purpose AI assistants for brainstorming, explanation of concepts, coding and writing support

:::

# Social Care Use Cases

## Social Care Use Cases

<br>
<br>

AI can transform the quality of life for both carers and residents:

| Technology | Use Case | Benefit |
|----|-----|-------------|
| **Machine Learning** | Fall Prevention | Early intervention before injury |
| **Forecasting** | Planning Care Needs | Forecasing number of residents needing specific care in the next years |
| **Optimization** | Staff Scheduling | Matches skills to specific needs |
| **NLP** | Voice Assistants | Independence for limited-mobility users |
| **Vision/Sensor AI** | Gait Analysis | Tracking physical rehabilitation progress |
| **Generative AI** | Documentation | Automating care notes and reports prepared by carers (efficiency boost) |

---



## Example: Predicting Future Care Demand

<br>
<br>

::::{.columns}
::: {.column width="31%" .fragment}

**📥 Inputs**

- Historical daily bed occupancy
- New referrals per week
- Demographics, diagnosis mix
- Seasonality indicators (month, holidays)

:::
::: {.column width="3%" .fragment}
<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
$\rightarrow$
:::

::: {.column width="31%" .fragment}

::: {style="border-left: 1px solid #6767677f; padding-left: 20px;"}

**📊 AI Model**

- Machine Learning Algorithm (e.g., Random Forest, LSTM Neural Network)
    - Learns patterns from historical data to identify relationships
:::
:::
::: {.column width="3%" .fragment}
<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
$\rightarrow$
:::

::: {.column width="31%" .fragment}
::: {style="border-left: 1px solid #6767677f; padding-left: 20px;"}
**📤Outputs**


- Predicted bed occupancy for the next months
- Confidence intervals for predictions to quantify uncertainty
- Risk of over/understaffing based on predicted demand
- Actionable insights for resource planning (e.g., "Increase staffing by 20% in December")
:::
:::
::::

# Critical Considerations


## Ethical and Practical Considerations for AI in Social Care

<br>
<br>

- **Transparency:** Explainable AI for accountability
- **Privacy:** Secure data handling and consent
- **Equity:** Avoiding bias in vulnerable populations
- **Human-in-the-Loop:** Final decisions remain with care professionals

# AI is a Tool to Assist, Not Replace !!!
